(d) **international 3PL footpring compounds with Move #11 international-expansion** (research/04 + playbook 11 + asset 13); Path C's international 3PL enables 2-3 day ship time to EU + UK + CA + AU + JP vs 5-10 day direct-from-US shipping = $500k-$5M Year-1 incremental cross-border revenue. (e) **the canonical 3PL-selection recipe is to RFQ to 5+ 3PLs** (Pitfall #2); operators should NOT sign with the first 3PL they contact.

1P recommended for $10M+ GMV) → FBA inbound creation + SKU labeling → listing creation + A+ content + Brand Store → Amazon Sponsored Products + Sponsored Brands launch ($80/day) → Vine review acquisition ($200/SKU + $600-1,500 product cost; 10-20 verified reviews in 30 days) → Buy Box ownership >90% + Amazon DSP branded-keyword defense ($500-2,000/mo via managed-service Tinuiti + Perpetual Traffic + CPC Strategy) → Triple Whale Marketplace Sync + Amazon Attribution + post-purchase email attribution setup (the 3 attribution stitches that prevent Amazon-Halo over-crediting)]; **(b) Phase 2 — Amazon + Walmart (US Amazon 3P+FBA + US Walmart 3P+WFS) ~20 hr cumulative Weeks 5–12** [Walmart Marketplace enrollment (2-4 week approval + NEW listings required) → WFS fulfillment setup ($3-8/unit pick-pack + 2-day shipping badge) → Walmart Sponsored Products + Walmart Connect launch ($45/day; Walmart CPC ~50-70% lower than Amazon per CPC Strategy 2024) → Walmart Buy Box ownership >85% + brand-canary-defense on Walmart (Walmart Brand Registry 4-8 week approval) → Attribution-merge Phase 2 (Triple Whale Walmart sync + Walmart Connect attribution + extended post-purchase email) → Phase 2 verification + steady-state monitoring (Monday morning weekly cadence)]; $10k-30k one-time + $2k-10k/yr) → bol + Zalando + Cdiscount enrollment + cross-listing automation via Sellbrite/Codisto/LitCommerce ($30-100/mo) → Amazon JP enrollment + JP-language listings + JP-specific compliance (PSE mark + Food Sanitation Act + JP customs) → EU compliance stack audit + GPSR renewal ($1k-5k/yr Responsible-Person appointment)]; **(d) Phase 4 — Target Plus + Walmart International + steady-state ~30 hr Quarter 2+ + dedicated marketplace manager** [Target Plus Roundel vendor application (6-12 month approval + 40-55% wholesale discount + 1P-via-Roundel model) → Walmart International CA + MX enrollment → Steady-state marketplace operations (daily monitoring + weekly review + monthly optimization + quarterly business review across all 14+ marketplaces)]. ## [2026-06-25 11:50] Move #8 — Loyalty program (Smile.io / Yotpo Loyalty / LoyaltyLion) playbook (5 paths, 6 steps, 7-gate verification)

- **What shipped:** `playbooks/07-loyalty-program-smile.md` (370-line paste-ready loyalty runbook — 5-path vendor decision matrix [A: Shopify ≤$1M → Smile.io Growth $249/mo, B: Shopify $1M–$10M on Yotpo → Yotpo Loyalty Starter $119/mo, C: Shopify $10M+ → Smile Enterprise / LoyaltyLion Pro, D: non-Shopify → LoyaltyLion Standard, E: custom → skip], 10-prereq gate including 5k-customer default + Triple Whale attribution + refund-policy documentation, 6-step build [install → points/tiers/referrals config → Klaviyo webhook wiring (the critical integration step) → 6-touch launch sequence (3 email + 3 SMS) → on-site widgets (post-purchase embed + floating widget + homepage banner) → 7-gate verification], 15-pitfall list with corrective "Fix:" lines, 7-step verification gate [A–G: app connected / Klaviyo webhook / tier-up event / launch sequence trigger / referral end-to-end / birthday reward / Triple Whale attribution], 15-metric monitoring table, cost-ROI table for default 5k-customer case [$249/mo Smile vs $1,968–$2,494/mo incremental margin = 7.9:1–10.0:1 net ROI, 1.5–2-week payback]). Pure playbook shape per Move #1/4/5/6/7 convention — Smile/Yotpo/LoyaltyLion UIs are the build surface, no script needed (cost math is operator-readable from the table).
- **Why this move:** Move #8 from `research/02-top-10-leverage-moves.md` and the prior tick (Move #7)'s named next-action. SMS-4 (Post-Purchase Review Request) is the natural on-ramp to a loyalty program — Move #7 establishes the post-purchase SMS touchpoint that a loyalty program hooks into (a 100-pt loyalty bonus on review submission is the seamless bridge between SMS-4 and loyalty enrollment). Default inputs (5k existing customers, $75 AOV, 70% margin) yield +5 pts repeat-purchase at 30d, +7 pts at 90d, +10% AOV on VIP orders, ~25 referral-driven first orders/mo = $1,968–$2,494/mo incremental margin at $249/mo Smile cost. Mid-tier brands typically scale to 10–15:1 net ROI by month 6 once the launch sequence has enrolled most of the customer base. **Honest-read pitfall called out**: loyalty has the longest ramp of any retention lever — the first month is typically neutral (cost incurred, no measurable lift); ROI materializes months 2–3+. Operators who can't commit to 60 days of post-launch measurement shouldn't launch (will quit at week 3 and abandon a program that would have paid back 10×).

- **What shipped:** `playbooks/05-migrate-to-klaviyo-postscript.md` (237-line paste-ready migration runbook — 4-path decision matrix [A: Mailchimp → Klaviyo+Postscript, B: Klaviyo+Attentive → Klaviyo+Postscript, C: Sendlane/Brevo/HubSpot/etc. → Klaviyo+Postscript, D: Klaviyo-only → Klaviyo+Postscript add-SMS], 6 sequential cutover phases [Audit & inventory → Build destination → Import contacts → Stand up Postscript → Cutover & monitor → Sunset source], 11-prereq gate, 13-pitfall list, 13-metric monitoring table, 7-step verification gate, cost-ROI table for default 25k-contact Mailchimp migration, 3 next-move pointers). Default case: 25k contacts Mailchimp Standard ($350/mo) → Klaviyo+Postscript ($400/mo) is +$50/mo on platform fees but unlocks +$300–$500/mo incremental revenue from better segmentation + new SMS flows (welcome + cart-abandon), so net ROI is +20% effective on email+SMS spend. Bigger stores (100k+ contacts) typically see −30 to −50% ESP cost savings on top of the revenue lift.

Every numbered step has a concrete action/command/decision point (no "set up your account" hand-waving); concrete named tools/values: 100 Klaviyo refs, 51 Postscript refs, 22 Mailchimp refs, 6 SPF, 5 DKIM, 5 DMARC, 8 TCPA, 5 10DLC refs. Every pitfall has a corrective action in the "Fix:" line. Cost table has 4 cost lines + delta + revenue lift + net ROI. ## [2026-06-24 11:25] Move #3 — Checkout audit + Baymard fix-list (playbook + scoring script)

- **What shipped:** `playbooks/03-checkout-audit-baymard.md` (251-line paste-ready runbook — 24-item audit checklist across 5 sections [Account & friction / Layout & form / Payment options / Trust & confidence / Mobile-specific], 7-step audit+fix loop, 12 common pitfalls, 4-step verification gate) and `scripts/checkout_audit_score.py` + `scripts/tests/test_checkout_audit_score.py` (working 0–100 scoring tool with severity-weighted points [L=5, M=3, S=1, E=0], 6-band health classifier [weak/fair/good/great/top_tier/missing], pass/partial/fail/skip statuses, prioritized fix-list ordered L-first, capped cumulative CVR lift at 80%, --input/--all-pass/--json CLI). 46/46 TDD tests green; 26/26 upsell + 15/15 abandoned-cart regression suites still green (87 total, 0 regressions).
- **Why this move:** Move #3 from `research/02-top-10-leverage-moves.md` — single biggest available lever for most DTC stores (Baymard: 65% of checkouts "mediocre or worse"; +35% CVR available from typical overhaul; mobile CVR is 46% of desktop and Shop Pay/Apple Pay typically lifts mobile +20–40%). Operator can self-audit in ~30 min, ship the top 3 L-severity fixes in 1–2 weeks, and re-score. Realistic mid-tier audit input produces score 54 ("fair") with 12 prioritized fixes and 28.7–66.5% expected CVR lift — immediately usable by an operator on Monday morning.
- **Files touched:** `playbooks/03-checkout-audit-baymard.md` (new, 251 lines), `scripts/checkout_audit_score.py` (new, ~430 lines), `scripts/tests/test_checkout_audit_score.py` (new, 46 tests across 11 test classes), `research/02-top-10-leverage-moves.md` (status tracker: Move #3 → shipped).

CLI runs end-to-end on a realistic 24-line audit input and produces a sensible prioritized fix-list. JSON output roundtrips through `json.load`. Both regression suites green (no regressions). Playbook's verification section (Steps A-D) is runnable end-to-end and asserts 4 gates.

Companion script should follow the same shape as `abandoned_cart_roi.py`: a `welcome_series_roi.py` that forecasts cost vs revenue for the 5-email series, plus a `playbooks/04-welcome-series-klaviyo.md` runbook. Surfaces the playbook's value proposition on the page that already lists it, so the operator clicks Playbook-01 having already seen the math work for their numbers.

- **What shipped:** A new "Your store — feed the ROI calculators" card on the Overview page (`/`) that captures AOV, monthly orders, and gross margin, persists them to localStorage as `ecom-ops:your-store:v1`, and broadcasts the values into the abandoned-cart, post-purchase upsell, and welcome-series calculators on `/playbooks`. The shared library `dashboard/src/lib/your-store.ts` exposes `loadYourStore` / `saveYourStore` / `mergeFromYourStore` so each calculator can project AOV → `aov` or `baseAov`, monthly orders → `checkoutsPerMonth` / `ordersPerMonth` / `optinsPerMonth`, and gross margin → `grossMargin` / `upsellMargin` without knowing which keys it shares. Each calculator now shows a "Prefilled from Your store on Overview" pill when its own localStorage was empty and Your-store filled the gap. Overview also shows live preview tiles for abandoned-cart ($X/mo recovered), post-purchase upsell (+$Y/mo net lift), and welcome-series ($Z/mo gross margin) derived from the current inputs. The "Reset everything" button on the card clears `your-store` AND the three per-calculator keys in one confirm.
- **Why this move:** Roadmap P1 ("state persistence across pages") + the exact next-action from the 2026-07-03 post-purchase tick: "wire a 'Your numbers' AOV/orders input panel on Overview (localStorage), then have the upsell + abandoned-cart + welcome-series calculators read those defaults on mount. One-touch personalization across all three ROI tools." Before, operators typed the same AOV into three separate calculators. Now they type it once on Overview and every calculator picks it up — and a small accent pill on each card tells them where the numbers came from (their inputs vs canonical defaults).

## 2026-07-06 06:13 UTC — My shipped playbooks tracker (state-persistence + cross-page-intelligence)

With 25 playbooks shipped and 5 Top-10 moves remaining, the operator had no way to remember which plays were live in their store. Now they toggle once and the progress persists across sessions — and the Overview page surfaces the count on every visit. Each shipped playbook doubles as a deployment-log entry because of the optional notes field, so an operator can answer "when did I ship Move #4?" or "which playbooks had first-week CVR lift ≥+20%?" without grepping a spreadsheet. Bounded: localStorage only, no server route, no new dependency.

## 2026-07-06 12:49 UTC — Personalized 30-day plan generator on /30-day-plan

- **What shipped:** A "Your 30-day plan — personalized to your store" card on `/30-day-plan` that reads Your-store (AOV / monthly orders / gross margin) + Shipped-playbooks (which playbooks the operator marked shipped) and produces a 4-week × 5-day calendar with verification gates and expected-lift annotations per task. One-click **Generate** button → 30-day schedule with weekend buffer days, projected monthly revenue, projected $ lift (low/high — capped at +80% to match the checkout-audit cap), and a per-move "moves in flight" pill that deep-links back to `/playbooks#<playbook-id>`. Two output actions: **Copy markdown** (paste-ready 80-line plan with Inputs, Projected lift, Rationale, and full 30-day Calendar sections) and **Download .md** (saves `30-day-plan-YYYY-MM-DD.md` to disk). The generator auto-skips any move already in `ecom-ops:shipped-playbooks:v1` and remaps the remaining queue — an operator who's shipped 5 moves gets a sharper, higher-leverage 30 days than someone starting from zero.
- **Why this move:** Roadmap P1 ("Generate 30-day launch plan" button that reads the operator's AOV/CAC inputs and produces a calendar-ready markdown checklist) + the natural complement to Your-store (P1) and Shipped-playbooks (P1). With 10 Top-10 moves queued and ~28 playbooks shipped, the operator had no way to say "given my numbers and what I've already shipped, what's the next 30 days look like?" — the static doc on `/30-day-plan` was great background reading but didn't account for per-operator state. The generator bridges the gap: same canonical 4-week sequence (Foundation → Conversion → Data+Channels → Compounding), but skips already-shipped moves and projects personalized $ lift from Your-store's monthly revenue.
- **Files touched:** `dashboard/src/lib/thirty-day-plan.ts` (new, ~480 lines — `MoveTemplate` / `PlanTask` / `PlanDay` / `PlanSummary` types + 10-move canonical template list (each with 4-6 tasks + verify gates + estLift strings), `MOVE_LIFT` per-move lift table, `generateThirtyDayPlan(inputs, shippedPlaybooks, now)` greedy scheduler that allocates tasks 3-per-workday across 30 calendar days with weekend buffer days, `renderPlanMarkdown(days, summary, inputs)` paste-ready markdown formatter with embedded deep-links back to `/playbooks#<id>`), `dashboard/src/components/thirty-day-plan.tsx` (new, ~360 lines — `"use client"` `ThirtyDayPlanGenerator` with input-status pre-flight (3 cards: Your-store detected / Shipped-playbooks count / Output format), Generate/Regenerate button, 4-tile summary strip (monthly revenue · planned moves · projected lift low/high), Copy markdown + Download .md actions, per-day `DayRow` with weekend-tinted background + move-in-flight pills that deep-link to `/playbooks`), `dashboard/app/30-day-plan/page.tsx` (modified — `ThirtyDayPlanGenerator` import + new `border-2` card inserted between the TL;DR card and the static 30-day plan tables; preserves the static synthesis doc as background reading), `docs/journal.md` (this entry).
- **Where to see it:** https://ecommerce-ops-iota.vercel.app/30-day-plan — scroll past the TL;DR card. The new card sits above the static 30-day plan tables. With Your-store empty (defaults) and no shipped playbooks: click **Generate my 30-day plan** → see 30-day calendar starting Day 1 (Mon) with Move #01 abandoned-cart (Week 1 · Foundation), Move #03 checkout-audit, Move #04 welcome-series, then Move #02 post-purchase upsell in Week 2, Move #05 Klaviyo+Postscript migration, etc. With Your-store populated ($95 AOV × 800 orders/mo) and 2 playbooks marked shipped: same click → plan skips Move #01 + Move #04, projects +75%–+80% monthly revenue lift (≈ +$57k–$60k/mo from $76k base), and renders a 30-day calendar with all 8 remaining moves packed into the first 18 working days.
- **Verification:** `npm run build` succeeds (✓ Compiled successfully, all 28 routes prerendered including `/30-day-plan` 8.61 kB up from 170 B static). `vercel deploy --prod` succeeded (Aliased https://ecommerce-ops-iota.vercel.app, Ready in 38s). JS chunk `chunks/app/30-day-plan/page-ba37ce9d8ef44e9e.js` contains the strings `Generate my 30-day plan`, `Regenerate`, `Copy markdown`, `Download .md`, `ecom-ops:your-store`, `ecom-ops:shipped-playbooks`, and the symbol `projectedLiftDollarsLow`. Planner math sanity-check: with `aov=95, monthlyOrders=800, grossMargin=0.65` and 2 shipped moves, the generator reports `plannedCount=8`, `projectedLiftLow=0.75`, `projectedLiftHigh=0.80` (capped at 80%), `projectedLiftDollarsLow=$57,000/mo`, `projectedLiftDollarsHigh=$60,800/mo` — math is byte-identical to the manual sum of `MOVE_LIFT` entries. ## 2026-07-07 06:48 UTC — Live Ikas data on Overview (live-data-integration + one-click-action + state-persistence)

- **What shipped:** A new "Your store at a glance" card on Overview (`/`) that pulls the partner's actual store data from the Ikas Admin GraphQL and surfaces it next to the existing Your-store inputs. The server route `/api/ikas/overview` (Next.js 15 Route Handler, `dynamic = "force-dynamic"`) reads the partner OAuth token from `process.env.IKAS_PARTNER_TOKEN` first, then `/data/.ikas/config.json` (verified schema: `getMerchant { id email firstName lastName merchantName storeName }` + `listOrder { count data { id orderNumber totalPrice currency orderedAt } }` + `listProduct { count }` probed 2026-07-07 against `https://api.myikas.com/api/v1/admin/graphql`). The route is server-rendered, so the access token never leaves the VPS / Vercel server. Summary stats (orders last 30d, revenue last 30d, average order value, active product count) flow into the client card, which renders four stat tiles (orders/revenue/AOV/products) and a "Apply to Your-store" button that writes the projected AOV + monthly-orders directly into the existing `ecom-ops:your-store:v1` localStorage key — so clicking once on Overview feeds every ROI calculator on `/playbooks`, the 30-day plan, and the next-move recommendation in one step. On expired / missing-config / network errors the card renders a typed reason (`expired` / `missing-config` / `network` / `graphql-error` / `io-error`) with a copy-pasteable `ikas auth login` recipe and a one-click Retry instead of fabricated numbers.
- **Why this move:** Roadmap P0 #1 exactly: "Live Ikas integration — wire a data source (Ikas GraphQL, file system, env vars, search index) into a page so users see their own numbers, not just industry medians." This was also the recommended next-action from the 2026-07-06 18:34 next-move-recommender journal: "(b) live Ikas GraphQL integration that auto-fills Your-store from real `getMerchant` / `listOrder` data when the operator has connected their store (P0 from the original roadmap, gated on `/data/.ikas/config.json` having a valid accessToken)." With Your-store + Shipped-playbooks + 30-day-plan + Next-move all in localStorage, the only remaining piece was "what does the operator's actual store look like?" — and the answer is now live. The integration deliberately tolerates the partner-token-expired case so the wiring ships today and turns green automatically the moment the operator drops a fresh `accessToken` into either the Vercel env (`IKAS_PARTNER_TOKEN`) or the VPS config file.
- **Files touched:** `dashboard/src/lib/ikas.ts` (new, ~270 lines — server-only Ikas GraphQL client + types + 30-day-window math + token loader that prefers `process.env.IKAS_PARTNER_TOKEN` over `/data/.ikas/config.json` for Vercel + local-fallback), `dashboard/src/lib/ikas-ui.ts` (new, ~55 lines — UI-safe response types + `formatIkasRevenue` mirror of `format.ts` so the client bundle stays free of `node:fs` / `node:path` imports), `dashboard/app/api/ikas/overview/route.ts` (new, ~22 lines — Next.js 15 Route Handler exporting `GET` only, HTTP 200 on success / HTTP 503 on a typed failure envelope, `cache-control: no-store`), `dashboard/src/components/ikas-live-card.tsx` (new, ~340 lines — `"use client"` card with 4 stat tiles + reload button + apply-to-Your-store button + typed error panel with refresh recipe per reason), `dashboard/app/page.tsx` (modified — 1 import + 1 new `<section id="ikas-overview">` block between the Your-store card and the next-move section; preserves all prior sections unchanged), `dashboard/src/lib/content.json` (regenerated by `node scripts/parse-content.mjs` — unchanged envelope, refresh is precautionary).
- **Where to see it:** https://ecommerce-ops-iota.vercel.app/#ikas-overview — scroll past the Your-store card. The live card shows "Your store at a glance" with a pulsing-green "Live Ikas data" badge, four tiles (Orders · 30d / Revenue · 30d / Avg order value / Active products), and a single "Apply to Your-store" action. With a valid token in `/data/.ikas/config.json` (or `IKAS_PARTNER_TOKEN`) the tiles render numbers like `$42,310 / 312 orders / $135 / 28 products` and clicking apply wires AOV=$135 + monthlyOrders=312 · 30d-projection across every calculator on `/playbooks` in one click. With the current expired token (or no env var), the same card renders the "Ikas data unavailable" panel with the reason + a 4-step `ikas auth login` recipe and a Retry button that re-attempts the API on click.
- **Verification:** `npm run build` succeeds (◉ Compiled successfully in 12.8s, 30 static routes prerendered, 1 dynamic route `/api/ikas/overview` registered; `/` is 7.96 kB up from 5.39 kB; chunks bundle the new `ecom-ops:your-store:v1` write path + the `loadIkasStoreOverview` symbol). `vercel deploy --prod` succeeded (Aliased https://ecommerce-ops-iota.vercel.app, Ready in 40s). Live HTML probe: `/` returns HTTP 200 (72,122 bytes) and contains the markers `ikas-live` (the section container id), `ikas-overview` (the anchor), `Live Ikas data` (the badge label), and `Your store at a glance` (the card title). Live `/api/ikas/overview` probe returns HTTP 503 with `{"ok":false,"reason":"missing-config","message":"No Ikas config at config.json..."}` — the truthful answer when the partner token isn't bundled into the Vercel deployment; the route documents both code paths (`process.env.IKAS_PARTNER_TOKEN` primary, file fallback) so setting the Vercel env var turns the same deployment green without code changes. The 4-step refresh recipe in the error panel mirrors `ikas auth login · PKCE OAuth · cp /data/.ikas/config.json /tmp/ikas-config.bak · paste the new accessToken · reload`, matching the canonical recipe documented in `docs/ikas-integration-discovery.md`. Type-check passes (the build runs `next build` with TypeScript validation; the only ESLint warning is the pre-existing "ESLint must be installed in order to run during builds" message present on every prior tick — not a regression).
- **Next action:** The wire is in; the live numbers are gated on a fresh partner token. Natural follow-up ticks once the token is refreshed: (a) add a "Your last-30-day revenue trend" mini-chart next to the four stat tiles using `listOrder`'s per-order `orderedAt + totalPrice` stream; (b) wire the abandoned-cart-AOV + welcome-series-AOV `expectedOrderValue` defaults to the live `averageOrderValueUsd` from this same card instead of `YOUR_STORE_DEFAULTS.aov`; (c) extend the live card with a "best-selling products" tile that pulls `listProduct { count data { id name } }` and surfaces the top 5 by `searchProducts` relevance. All three are one-tick features that compound the integration without needing a new npm dependency.
